2604.12875.title: AISafetyBenchExplorer: A Metric-Aware Catalogue of AI Safety Benchmarks Reveals Fragmented Measurement and Weak Benchmark Governance
2604.12875.paper_summary: The rapid expansion of large language model (LLM) safety evaluation has produced a substantial benchmark ecosystem, but not a correspondingly coherent measurement ecosystem. We present AISafetyBenchExplorer, a structured catalogue of 195 AI safety benchmarks released between 2018 and 2026, organized through a multi-sheet schema that records benchmark-level metadata, metric-level definitions, benchmark-paper metadata, and repository activity. This design enables meta-analysis not only of what benchmarks exist, but also of how safety is operationalized, aggregated, and judged across the literature. Using the updated catalogue, we identify a central structural problem: benchmark proliferation has outpaced measurement standardization. The current landscape is dominated by medium-complexity benchmarks (94/195), while only 7 benchmarks occupy the Popular tier. The workbook further reports strong concentration around English-only evaluation (165/195), evaluation-only resources (170/195), stale GitHub repositories (137/195), stale Hugging Face datasets (96/195), and heavy reliance on arXiv preprints among benchmarks with known venue metadata. At the metric level, the catalogue shows that familiar labels such as accuracy, F1 score, safety score, and aggregate benchmark scores often conceal materially different judges, aggregation rules, and threat models. We argue that the field's main failure mode is fragmentation rather than scarcity. Researchers now have many benchmark artifacts, but they often lack a shared measurement language, a principled basis for benchmark selection, and durable stewardship norms for post publication maintenance. AISafetyBenchExplorer addresses this gap by providing a traceable benchmark catalogue, a controlled metadata schema, and a complexity taxonomy that together support more rigorous benchmark discovery, comparison, and meta-evaluation.
2604.12875.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLM）安全評估的快速擴展產生了一個可觀的基準生態系統，但並未形成相應一致的測量生態系統。我們提出了AISafetyBenchExplorer，一個結構化的目錄，包含2018年至2026年間發布的195個AI安全基準，通過多工作表架構組織，記錄基準級元數據、指標級定義、基準論文元數據和倉庫活動。這一設計不僅使得對現有基準的元分析成為可能，還能分析安全在文獻中是如何被操作化、聚合和評判的。利用更新的目錄，我們識別出一個核心結構性問題：基準的增長速度超過了測量標準化的速度。目前的格局由中等複雜度的基準主導（94/195），而只有7個基準位於流行層級。工作簿進一步報告了強烈集中於僅限英語的評估（165/195）、僅限評估的資源（170/195）、過時的GitHub倉庫（137/195）、過時的Hugging Face數據集（96/195），以及在擁有已知場地元數據的基準中對arXiv預印本的重度依賴。在指標層面上，目錄顯示，像準確率、F1分數、安全分數和聚合基準分數這樣熟悉的標籤，往往隱藏了實質上不同的評判者、聚合規則和威脅模型。我們認為，該領域的主要失敗模式是碎片化而非稀缺。研究人員現在擁有許多基準文物，但他們往往缺乏共享的測量語言、基準選擇的原則基礎，以及發布後維護的持久管理規範。AISafetyBenchExplorer通過提供可追溯的基準目錄、受控的元數據架構和複雜性分類法來填補這一空白，這三者共同支持更嚴謹的基準發現、比較和元評估。
2604.12875.author: Abiodun A. Solanke et.al.
